title: Simple Dal Tadka
description: A classic, comforting, and frugal Indian lentil soup. Easy to make and very nutritious.
explanation: Dal tadka is a base recipe more than a single fixed dish. Once the dal is soft, the final taste comes from the tempering, the thickness, and what you serve it with.
Use this as a dependable protein-and-fiber side. Make it thinner for rice, thicker for roti, and more vegetable-heavy when you want a fuller bowl.
ingredients: 1 cup Yellow Moong Dal (split yellow lentils)
instructions: Wash the dal thoroughly. Boil the dal in water with turmeric and salt until soft and mushy (about 15-20 minutes).
In a small pan, heat oil. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add minced garlic (if using) and saute until golden.
Pour this tadka (tempering) over the cooked dal.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve with rice or roti.
stepNotes: Moong dal cooks quickly and feels light. Masoor, toor, or mixed dal also work; just adjust water and cooking time.
Use mustard seeds, curry leaves, dried red chilli, or hing here if that suits your kitchen.
Skip garlic for a Jain-style or no-onion/no-garlic version. Ginger, hing, cumin, and tomato can still give plenty of flavor.
Simmer for a minute after adding tadka if you want the flavor to blend through the dal.
For a complete meal, pair with rice or roti plus a vegetable sabzi, salad, or pickle.

variationSections.title: Thickness and serving style
items: For rice, keep the dal pourable and slightly thinner.
For roti, simmer longer so the dal is thicker and easier to scoop.
For soup, add more water, vegetables, lemon, and black pepper.

variationSections.title: Flavor variations
items: Tomato dal: cook chopped tomato with the tadka before adding it to dal.
Spinach dal: add chopped spinach in the last few minutes of simmering.
Richer dal: finish with tahini, cashew paste, or a small amount of coconut milk instead of ghee.
